_meta.source: Repair manuals, YouTube pull videos, eBay listing photos
_meta.confidence: High for engine bay, Medium for interior modules, Verify for trim-specific parts

instrument cluster.location: Behind dashboard, center of steering column
instrument cluster.access: Remove trim bezel (snap clips), 2-4 screws, disconnect harness
instrument cluster.notes: Watch for hidden screws behind dash trim. Some require steering wheel drop.

ECU.location: Engine bay passenger side firewall OR behind glove box
ECU.access: Remove plastic cover (clips) OR remove glove box (2 screws)
ECU.notes: Location varies by make. Honda/Acura: passenger kick panel. Toyota: engine bay. GM: engine bay.

ABS module.location: Engine bay, mounted to master cylinder/brake booster
ABS module.access: Remove cover, disconnect brake lines (do NOT mix up!)
ABS module.notes: Brake fluid will leak. Bring rags. Do NOT cross-thread lines on removal.

throttle body.access: Remove intake hose, 4 bolts, disconnect harness
throttle body.notes: Very accessible. Often has coolant lines attached (pinch clamps).

amplifier.location: Trunk/rear cargo area OR under passenger seat OR behind glove box
amplifier.access: Remove trunk panel clips OR lift seat forward OR remove glove box
amplifier.notes: Bose/BOSE: often in trunk. Harman Kardon: under seat. Mark Levinson: trunk or rear deck. Verify with trunk panel removal first.

BCM.location: Under driver dash OR behind glove box OR left of steering column
BCM.access: Remove lower dash panel (2-3 screws), locate module with multiple harnesses
BCM.notes: Usually black box with 3-5 large connectors. Label connectors with tape before removal.

TCM.location: Passenger footwell OR engine bay on transmission OR under center console
TCM.access: Remove kick panel OR transmission cover OR console trim
TCM.notes: Often integrated with ECU on newer cars. Check eBay listing photos to confirm separate module.

HID ballast.location: Under headlight assembly OR behind bumper cover OR near headlight base
HID ballast.access: Open hood, look for rectangular box attached to headlight wiring
HID ballast.notes: Small black box ~3x5 inches. Often attached to headlight housing or frame rail. Don't confuse with headlight module.

climate control.location: Center dash behind radio OR integrated with radio bezel
climate control.access: Remove radio trim (snap clips), 4 screws, disconnect harness
climate control.notes: Often includes blend door actuators. Pull the whole panel as assembly.

fuel pump.location: Top of fuel tank, under rear seat OR trunk floor panel
fuel pump.access: Lift rear seat (clips) OR remove trunk panel, disconnect fuel lines
fuel pump.notes: Tank may be 1/4 full = heavy. Fuel vapors = NO SPARKS. Ring retainer may be rusted.

turbocharger.location: Exhaust manifold side, center of engine bay
turbocharger.access: Remove heat shield, intake tubing, exhaust flange bolts
turbocharger.notes: Rusty exhaust bolts = PB Blaster first. Heavy part. Oil/coolant lines attached.

seat control.location: Under driver seat OR integrated into seat base
seat control.access: Slide seat all the way back, look underneath for small black box
seat control.notes: Memory seat cars always have module. Base models may not. Check for heated seat buttons as indicator.

alternator.location: Front of engine, driven by serpentine belt
alternator.notes: Very accessible on most cars. Note belt routing before removal.

MAF sensor.location: Intake duct between air filter and throttle body
MAF sensor.access: Unclip sensor from intake tube, disconnect harness
MAF sensor.notes: Easiest pull on the list. Don't touch sensor wires.

radio nav.access: Remove dash trim (clips), 4 mounting screws, disconnect harness/antenna
radio nav.notes: Premium nav units often have separate DVD drive below. Pull both. GPS antenna on dash top.
